title: sorbus aucuparia f. integerrima
intro: Sorbus aucuparia f. integerrima, also known as the European mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to mountainous regions in Europe and Asia, characterized by its white flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of sorbic acid and other bioactive compounds. Consuming the plant's berries has been reported to have various health benefits, such as boosting the immune system, reducing oxidative stress, and potentially lowering cholesterol levels. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the flowers, leaves, or berries and then infusing them in hot water or mixing them with a solvent.
